title: ericameria fasciculata
intro: Ericameria fasciculata, also known as the bundle goldenrod, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It belongs to the sunflower family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, with its health benefits including reducing inflammation and fighting infections. Herbal preparations of Ericameria fasciculata can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
